bullet_points: Compute and cache global per-channel mean and standard deviation across all 1523 patches (without noise) to disk.
Implement a custom torch.utils.data.Dataset class that loads these cached statistics at initialization.

Enforce the noise sampling strategy: SO noise indices sampled randomly from 3000 realizations (independent of patch index); Planck noise indices sampled from 100 realizations with the patch index matching the sky patch index.
Implement data augmentation (random 90-degree rotations and flips).
Set up DataLoader with num_workers=8, pin_memory=True, and explicitly set OMP_NUM_THREADS=8 to prevent oversubscription.
Verify the pipeline by printing shapes and statistics of a sample batch.
Implement the multi-scale U-Net with gated cross-attention modules.
Define the dual-stream input: primary stream (90, 150, 217 GHz) as a 3-channel tensor; auxiliary CIB stream (353, 545, 857 GHz, converted to µK_CMB) as a separate 3-channel tensor.
Ensure gated cross-attention fuses these streams at each decoder level.
Verify forward pass with dummy tensors.
Print total trainable parameter count and estimate GPU memory footprint for a batch size of 16 to ensure it fits within 96 GB VRAM.
Implement the composite loss: L = λ1 L_L1 + λ2 L_FeatureMatching + λ3 L_Spectral.
Integrate utils.powers for the spectral consistency term, computed on a subset of the batch every 10 iterations to maintain efficiency.
Implement curriculum learning: Stage 1 (30% of total epochs) trains on high-intensity patches (max tSZ > 75th percentile); Stage 2 trains on the full dataset.
Execute training using AdamW and one-cycle LR scheduler with mixed-precision (FP16).
Save trained model weights and training loss history.
Run inference on the full test set and save results.
Perform the CIB-tSZ null test by shuffling CIB patch indices relative to tSZ ground truth and evaluating reconstruction performance.
Compute the global cross-correlation coefficient rℓ between reconstruction and ground truth.
Extract the Y_SZ-M relation using the patch-integrated tSZ signal as a proxy for cluster mass.
Calculate residual scatter as a function of mass and compare against raw SO-LAT data; save all metrics to disk.
sub_tasks.sub_task: CIB-tSZ Null Test and Scientific Validation

bullet_points: Generate a multi-panel figure comparing ground truth, raw SO-LAT input, and SR-DAE reconstruction for representative patches.
Generate a multi-panel figure showing radial power spectra (ground truth vs. reconstruction) and the Y_SZ-M relation with residual scatter.
Ensure all panels share consistent color scales and axis ranges.
Save all figures as high-resolution PNG files.
Synthesize quantitative results from validation and null tests.
Interpret SR-DAE performance in reconstructing baryonic pressure fluctuations.
Discuss the effectiveness of gated cross-attention and the spectral loss term in suppressing hallucinated power.
Quantify information gain over a matched-filter baseline, referencing rℓ at 1–5 arcmin scales.
Write the full Results section of the report, summarizing the scientific impact.
